commands.review_summary:
The branch adds update-mode handling in managed npm plugin installs to preserve newer exact direct dependency pins, plus regression tests for the managed root writer and npm-spec install path.

PR surface: Source +110, Tests +100. Total +210 across 4 files.

Reproducibility: yes. source-reproducible with high confidence: current main writes the requested dependency spec into the managed npm root, and the linked issue gives a concrete `openclaw update --tag` downgrade path. I did not run the mutating real update flow locally because this review is read-only.
commands.review_followup: Human/contributor follow-up is needed because the PR has a concrete code defect and missing contributor-side real behavior proof that automation cannot supply for the external branch.

target.title: fix(update): preserve newer managed plugin pins
target.body:
`openclaw update` could silently downgrade a user-pinned managed plugin direct dependency, reintroducing plugin hotfix bugs and leaving the managed npm root inconsistent with user intent.

Fixes #85184.

## Affected surface

- `src/plugins/install.ts`
  - `installPluginFromManagedNpmRoot`
  - managed npm update-mode resolution handling
- `src/infra/npm-managed-root.ts`
  - `upsertManagedNpmRootDependency`
  - managed root direct dependency spec reading
- Tests:
  - `src/plugins/install.npm-spec.test.ts`
  - `src/infra/npm-managed-root.test.ts`

## Scope

This is a narrow update-path guard for exact semver direct dependency pins. It does not introduce a new config flag and does not change general install-mode behavior.

- In `mode: "update"`, detect when the managed npm root already has a newer exact direct dependency spec than the update target.
- If that newer version is already installed, reuse the installed package instead of running `npm install` and downgrading it.
- If the package is pinned newer but not currently installed, keep the newer pin as the install/verification target so npm resolves the user-pinned version instead of the older bundled spec.
- Non-semver specs, equal versions, older pins, manual install mode, and unrelated dependency updates keep the existing behavior.

## Real behavior proof

### RED

Before the implementation, the install-path regression test failed because update mode still installed the older bundled version:

```text
FAIL |plugins| src/plugins/install.npm-spec.test.ts > installPluginFromNpmSpec > reuses a newer installed managed dependency during update instead of downgrading
Error: npm install resolved @martian-engineering/lossless-claw to version 0.10.0, expected 0.11.2
```

### GREEN

```text
$ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.infra.config.ts src/infra/npm-managed-root.test.ts
Test Files  1 passed (1)
Tests  19 passed (19)

$ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.plugins.config.ts src/plugins/install.npm-spec.test.ts
Test Files  1 passed (1)
Tests  38 passed (38)

$ node scripts/check-changed.mjs --base origin/main
[check:changed] lanes=core, coreTests
[check:changed] src/infra/npm-managed-root.test.ts: core test
[check:changed] src/infra/npm-managed-root.ts: core production
[check:changed] src/plugins/install.npm-spec.test.ts: core test
[check:changed] src/plugins/install.ts: core production
...
Import cycle check: 0 runtime value cycle(s).
(exit 0)

$ git diff --check origin/main..HEAD
(exit 0)

$ node scripts/check-no-conflict-markers.mjs
(exit 0)

$ gitleaks protect --staged --redact
no leaks found
```

## Coverage note

I re-ran the required no-collision searches immediately before pushing this branch; all returned no open PRs:

```text
gh search prs --repo openclaw/openclaw --state open "Fixes #85184"
gh search prs --repo openclaw/openclaw --state open "#85184"
gh search prs --repo openclaw/openclaw --state open "upsertManagedNpmRootDependency"
gh search prs --repo openclaw/openclaw --state open "npm-managed-root.ts downgrade"
gh search prs --repo openclaw/openclaw --state open "openclaw update pinned dependency"
```

Recent related work in this area covers managed peer dependencies and bundled plugin install planning, but it does not cover direct dependency downgrade prevention for an already-pinned managed npm root package.

## What was not tested

- I did not run a mutating real `openclaw update --tag` against a user home directory.
- I did not query npm for the live `@martian-engineering/lossless-claw` dist-tags during the test; the regression uses deterministic managed npm root fixtures.
